back_translation: 'You shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twisted linen, with blue and purple and scarlet yarn — with cherubim skillfully worked into them.'
'The length of one curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits, and the width four cubits; all the curtains shall have one measure.'
'Five of the curtains shall be joined to one another, and five curtains joined to one another.'
'You shall make loops of blue on the edge of the end curtain in the first set, and likewise on the edge of the outermost curtain in the second set.'
'Fifty loops you shall make on the one curtain, and fifty loops you shall make on the edge of the curtain that is in the second set, the loops opposite one another.'
'You shall make fifty gold clasps, and join the curtains one to another with the clasps, so that the tabernacle shall be one.'
'You shall make curtains of goats' hair for a tent over the tabernacle — eleven curtains shall you make them.'
'The length of one curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the width four cubits; one measure for the eleven curtains.'
'You shall join five of the curtains by themselves and six of the curtains by themselves; and you shall double over the sixth curtain at the front of the tent.'
'You shall make fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain in the first set, and fifty loops on the edge of the corresponding curtain in the second set.'
'You shall make fifty bronze clasps, and put the clasps through the loops, and join the tent together so that it is one.'
'And the overhang that remains of the tent curtains, the half curtain that is left over, shall hang over the back of the tabernacle.'
'And the cubit on this side and the cubit on that side, of what is left over in the length of the tent curtains, shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle on this side and on that, to cover it.'
'You shall make a covering for the tent of ram skins dyed red, and a covering of fine leather above.'
'You shall make the frames for the tabernacle of acacia wood, standing upright.'
'Ten cubits the length of the frame, and a cubit and a half cubit the width of one frame.'
'Two tenons for one frame, joined to one another; thus you shall make for all the frames of the tabernacle.'
'You shall make the frames for the tabernacle: twenty frames for the south side.'
'And forty silver bases shall you make under the twenty frames — two bases under one frame for its two tenons, and two bases under one frame for its two tenons.'
'For the second side of the tabernacle, the north side, twenty frames.'
'And their forty bases of silver — two bases under one frame, and two bases under one frame.'
'For the rear of the tabernacle westward, you shall make six frames.'
'And two frames you shall make for the corners of the tabernacle at the rear.'
'They shall be coupled from below, and together they shall be complete at the top to one ring; so it shall be for both of them; for the two corners they shall be.'
'So there shall be eight frames, and their silver bases, sixteen bases — two bases under one frame, and two bases under one frame.'
'You shall make crossbars of acacia wood — five for the frames of one side of the tabernacle,'
'and five crossbars for the frames of the second side of the tabernacle, and five crossbars for the frames of the side of the tabernacle, for the rear westward.'
'And the middle crossbar in the midst of the frames shall extend from end to end.'
'The frames you shall overlay with gold, and their rings you shall make of gold, holders for the crossbars; and you shall overlay the crossbars with gold.'
'You shall raise up the tabernacle according to its plan that was shown you on the mountain.'
'You shall make a veil of blue and purple and scarlet yarn and fine twisted linen; the work of a skilled craftsman shall make it, with cherubim.'
'You shall hang it on four pillars of acacia, overlaid with gold, their hooks of gold, on four silver bases.'
'You shall hang the veil under the clasps, and bring there from within the veil the ark of the Testimony; and the veil shall divide for you between the Holy Place and the Most Holy Place.'
'You shall put the mercy seat on the ark of the Testimony in the Most Holy Place.'
'You shall place the table outside the veil, and the lampstand opposite the table, on the south side of the tabernacle; and the table you shall put on the north side.'
'For the entrance of the tent you shall make a screen of blue and purple and scarlet yarn and fine twisted linen, the work of an embroiderer.'
'You shall make for the screen five pillars of acacia, and overlay them with gold; their hooks shall be of gold, and you shall cast for them five bronze bases.'
